description_long: This is a traditional stone windmill with a cylindrical tower and a conical, dark-shingled roof. The structure features a prominent wooden door at the front, flanked by a small stone step and a circular millstone leaning against the wall. Four large wooden lattice blades are attached to a central hub, extending outward and dominating the upper facade. The stonework of the tower is textured and weathered, suggesting age and durability, while small windows are sparsely distributed around the structure. The windmill's overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, typical of historical European rural or semi-urban landscapes.
description: A cylindrical stone windmill with a conical roof and four large wooden lattice blades, featuring a wooden door and a leaning millstone.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, a small step, and a millstone beside the door, with the windmill blades extending to the right.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the full span of the four windmill blades, with the door partially visible and the cylindrical stone tower prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view with the conical roof and stone tower visible, only the edge of the windmill blades and no door or millstone in sight.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the back of the tower, part of the roof, and the millstone on the side, with the windmill blades extending outward.
